EXAMINATION:  CT HEAD W/O CONTRAST.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with altered mental status  // Rule out bleed .

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images from skullbase to vertex were obtained
without intravenous contrast.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 5.0 s, 18.8 cm; CTDIvol = 45.5 mGy (Head) DLP =
855.5 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Head) = 856 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Noncontrast CT of the head from ___.
MR head from ___.

FINDINGS: 

Numerous scattered bilateral partially calcified lesions with extensive
surrounding vasogenic edema appear stable in number and size from ___. 
For example, in the right frontal lobe a 14 x 11 mm mass is unchanged from
___ (04:20).  Micro hemorrhage versus mineralization within the left
parietal lesion identified on MR from ___ appears unchanged from ___
(04:22).  No new areas of hemorrhage are identified.  Ventricles are unchanged
in size and configuration.  There is no midline shift.  The basal cisterns are
patent.  No osseous abnormalities seen. The paranasal sinuses, mastoid air
cells, and middle ear cavities are clear. The orbits are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:


1. No significant change from prior head CT dated ___, and no definite or
new areas of hemorrhage.
2. Unchanged scattered bilateral partially calcified lesions with extensive
vasogenic edema.
3. Multiple scattered metastatic lesions, with small area of microhemorrhage
versus mineralization in the left parietal region and vasogenic edema are
stable from ___.

EXAMINATION:  CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with widely metastatic adenocarcinoma of unknown
primary, complicated hospital course including HCAP. New fever  // Eval fever.
Eval progression of PNA

TECHNIQUE:  Portable chest radiograph

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior chest radiographs, most recently performed on ___ ; outside facility chest CT ___

FINDINGS: 

Dobhoff tube terminates in the body of the stomach.  Right subclavian line has
been removed in the interim.  SVC stent appears unchanged in position.

Bibasilar consolidations may represent a combination of pneumonia and/ or
atelectasis, not significantly changed.  Multiple bilateral pulmonary
metastases are known to the patient.  Small right pleural effusion has
decreased.  No pneumothorax.  Cardiomediastinal contours are stable.

Right hemidiaphragm is persistently elevated.

IMPRESSION:


1. Slight interval decrease in size of a small right pleural effusion, with
persistent bibasilar pneumonia and/or atelectasis.
2. Multiple bilateral pulmonary metastases.

EXAMINATION:  PA and lateral chest radiographs

INDICATION:  ___ with a history of recently diagnosedmetastatic adenocarcinoma
of unknown primary with brain, bone,liver metastases, SVC syndrome s/p
stenting and multiple UEthrombosis on lovenox, with hospital course notable
for pericardial effusion s/p pericardiocentesis, tx for PNA, and altered
mental status. Significant family meeting ___ -> pt is DNR/DNI.  // ?Pneumonia

TECHNIQUE:  Chest PA and lateral

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___.

FINDINGS: 

Right subclavian line has its tip in projected over the cavoatrial junction,
unchanged from prior.  Dobbhoff tube has its tip projecting over the stomach. 
There are  small-to-moderate bilateral pleural effusions, right greater than
left, unchanged from prior.  There has been interval improvement in the
diffuse haziness throughout both lungs likely represent improving pulmonary
edema.  There is persistent consolidative opacities in both lower lobes, that
could represent atelectasis, however pneumonia cannot be excluded. Mildly
enlarged cardiomediastinal silhouette is unchanged.

IMPRESSION: 

1.   Right subclavian line has its tip in projected over the cavoatrial
junction, unchanged from prior.
2. Dobbhoff tube has its tip projecting over the stomach.
3.  Unchanged small-to-moderate bilateral pleural effusions, right greater
than left, unchanged from prior.
3. Interval improvement in  diffuse bilateral pulmonary edema.
4. Persistent consolidative opacities in both lower l
[... report truncated ...]

INDICATION:  ___ with a history of recently diagnosedmetastatic adenocarcinoma
of unknown primary with brain, bone,liver metastases, SVC syndrome s/p
stenting and multiple UEthrombosis on lovenox, with hospital course notable
for pericardial effusion s/p pericardiocentesis, tx for PNA, and altered
mental status. Significant family meeting ___ -> pt is DNR/DNI. // For dobhoff
placement

TECHNIQUE:  Portable chest radiograph.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The Dobbhoff tube terminates in the stomach.  The right IJ central venous
catheter terminates in caval atrial junction.

Lung volume is small.  The right atelectasis and pleural effusion has
increased.  The left atelectasis is unchanged.  The left costophrenic angle is
out of view.  The lungs are otherwise clear.  The cardiac silhouette is
enlarged and unchanged.  The mediastinum is unchanged.

IMPRESSION: 

1.The Dobbhoff tube terminates in the stomach.

2.  Worsening right atelectasis and pleural effusion.

EXAMINATION:  CT HEAD W/O CONTRAST Q111 CT HEAD

INDICATION:  ___ with a history of recently diagnosedmetastatic adenocarcinoma
of unknown primary with brain, bone,liver metastases, SVC syndrome s/p
stenting and multiple UEthrombosis on lovenox now presenting with hypotension
___ PNA oncefepime/flagyl or ___ tamponade iso pericarditis and
pericardialeffusion requiring pericardiocentesis course c/b ___ and ___
altered mental status possibly ___ seizures from metastases (EEG shows
epileptiform discharges left paracentrally) on keppra and vimpat. Significant
family meeting ___ -> pt is DNR/DNI.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images of the brain were obtained without
contrast.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 5.0 s, 18.8 cm; CTDIvol = 45.5 mGy (Head) DLP =
855.5 mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 3.0 s, 11.4 cm; CTDIvol = 45.0 mGy (Head) DLP =
513.3 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Head) = 1,369 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT head ___.

FINDINGS: 

In comparison to the prior examination, innumerable, small lesions, measuring
up to 0.9 cm, many of which are partially calcified, primarily found at the
gray-white junction are unchanged in size and extent.  Multiple foci of
vasogenic edema worse in the left frontoparietal and right frontal lobes is
unchanged in size and extent.   There is no evidence of midline shift or
worsening mass effect.

There is no intracranial hemorrhage or large territory infarction.
